content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates wonder and empathy and tracks the emotional thread well, following the charge from numbers to the kitchen table with appropriate pacing. However, vulnerability is essentially absent — the coach never names their own experience, never offers 'maybe I'm off' or any first-person disclosure. More notably, impartiality wavers: turn 6 ('You have the choice right now') and turn 8 ('So the choice you're after — you already have it') have a declarative, slightly cornering quality that reads as the coach being a half-step ahead of the client with a point to make, rather than purely curious. Turn 10 ('that's not prudence') is insightful but also corrective — telling the client what their feeling is rather than wondering with them. The coach lands well at turns 12 and 14, where the stance softens into genuine presence ('Yeah. And it hasn't stopped, has it'). After turn 15, when the client tenses and retreats back to 'planning problem,' the conversation ends before we see whether the coach can stay undefended with that. Overall: empathy and wonder present, vulnerability missing, impartiality intermittent — squarely a 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Curious challenge, but slightly pointed — invites reflection rather than opens wonder."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean, open question from curiosity about the client's experience."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'You have the choice right now' is declarative and has an agenda flavor — coach is making a point, not wondering."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach summarizes and pivots; the 'so' construction signals coach is steering toward a destination they've already identified."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Interpretive ('that's not prudence... something older') — empathic read but told to client rather than wondered with."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Warmer, more present. Acknowledges client's protest without arguing, then asks an open question from genuine curiosity."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Yeah. And it hasn't stopped, has it.' — spacious, undefended, matches the client's quiet. Best VIEW moment in the call."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["lets-go-there", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome"],
  "notes": "Coach never makes themselves visible (no first-person state-naming). Stance is competent and tracking, but operates from a slightly knowing position rather than fully open wonder."
}
